commit.message:
Fix composite: tight smoothstep for clean mask separation

The smoothstep transition width was linked to EdgeSmooth (0.35-0.65
range), causing green BG color to bleed through onto the person.
Now uses a fixed tight transition (±0.075 around Threshold) so mask
values >0.575 are fully person and <0.425 are fully background.
EdgeSmooth only controls spatial blur in RenderMaskBlur.

commit.message:
rewrite(compress): switch to Ghostscript — drop pdf-lib entirely

pdf-lib is a pure-JavaScript PDF serialiser. Saving a 33 MB document
re-serialises every object in the file and blocks the Node.js event loop
for 5–10 minutes. That is the root cause of the 10-minute hang.

New approach: Ghostscript (native C++, installed in Dockerfile).
  - Handles CMYK/RGB/Gray images natively
  - Downsamples images to target DPI using Bicubic filter
  - -dConvertCMYKImagesToRGB reduces 4-ch CMYK → 3-ch sRGB (~25% bonus)
  - Runs in 60–120 s on a 33 MB scanned PDF (vs 5–10 min with pdf-lib)
  - Async-job pattern means no HTTP timeout regardless of duration

Level settings:
  low    200 DPI, QFactor 0.3  → ~30-50% reduction
  medium 150 DPI, QFactor 0.6  → ~50-70% reduction
  high   100 DPI, QFactor 1.2  → ~70-85% reduction

Also: update processing message to 'takes 1–2 minutes' (honest UX).

commit.message:
fix stale EE positions after auto-reset by forcing FK update in play.py

Isaac Lab step() auto-reset does not call write_data_to_sim()+sim.forward()
after _reset_idx(), so body transforms (EE positions from FrameTransformer)
are stale from the previous episode. This caused the model to think the arm
was still near the cube after reset, outputting stuck actions immediately.

commit.message:
Fix Web Push encryption: double 0x01 in HKDF info strings

The CEK and nonce info strings included a trailing \x01 byte, but
hkdfExpand() already appends \x01 per RFC 5869. This caused the
derived keys to be wrong, making the browser unable to decrypt the
push payload (silently dropped by the browser).

commit.message:
Fix: trasladar steel layers a coordenadas locales para secciones composite

Las steel layers de secciones composite venían en coordenadas globales
del modelo ETABS, pero el width_profile ya estaba en coordenadas locales
(0..h_total). Este desalineamiento causaba que _invert_section() produjera
posiciones negativas, generando 0 puntos M- en el diagrama P-M.

También corrige el M+ que estaba inflado ~2.5x por las posiciones
de acero desplazadas.

commit.message:
v1.0.5: NSIS installer, folder picker on first run, open-folder button

- Switch Windows target from portable → nsis + zip. NSIS creates a
  proper installer with Start Menu/desktop shortcuts and an uninstaller;
  eliminates the self-extracting portable exe that Windows Defender was
  treating as suspicious.
- First-run dialog (Electron dialog API) asks user to pick their project
  folder before anything else starts. Choice is saved in Electron userData
  (AppData\Roaming\…\prefs.json) and reused on every subsequent launch.
- "Change…" button in footer lets users switch project folders at any
  time; app relaunches automatically into the new location.
- "📁 Open" button in footer opens the project folder in Explorer/Finder
  via shell.openPath so users can easily access their layers and builds.
- server.js now deferred until USER_DATA_DIR env var is set, so all
  bootstrap mkdirSync calls write to the user-chosen folder.
- Add author/copyright metadata; update appId to com.tojiba.nftcompositor2.

commit.message:
fix: smart CSV path resolution for Render deployment

On Render, datasource.filepath contains Windows paths from the original
upload which don't exist on Linux. The fallback used UPLOAD_DIR/filename
but files are saved with timestamp prefixes (e.g. 1772419706_XAUUSD.csv)
while filename stores the original name (XAUUSD.csv).

Added _resolve_csv_path() helper that:
1. Tries original filepath
2. Falls back to UPLOAD_DIR/filename
3. Searches for files ending with filename (handles timestamp prefixes)

Applied to all 7 file resolution points in backtest.py.

commit.message:
Story selection: order by version ASC then review score DESC

- Add TitleRepository.update_review_id() (mirrors ContentRepository pattern)
- Steps 05, 07: set Title.review_id after saving title review
- Step 06: set Content.review_id after saving content review
- Steps 05-09: replace created_at FIFO with version ASC + score DESC selection
  - Step 05: ORDER BY t.version ASC, s.created_at ASC

commit.message:
feat: add AI-enhanced package update automation

Created a unified package update system that combines automation with
LLM-based error fixing. Key features:

- Nix-shell shebang for automatic dependency management
- Two-phase operation: scan for updates, then apply with testing
- Smart error handling: distinguishes hash fetch vs build failures
- LLM integration via Goose CLI for build error recovery
- GitHub API authentication support (60 or 5000 req/hour)
- Comprehensive logging and statistics

New files:
- update-packages-ai.sh: Main automation script (413 lines)
- UPDATE_WORKFLOW.md: Complete documentation
- .github/workflows/batch-update-packages.yml: Batch processing
- .github/workflows/update-single-package.yml: Manual updates

Removed old scripts:
- update-packages.sh (detection only)
- auto-update-packages.sh (basic automation)
- update-radvd.sh (one-off script)

All functionality consolidated into update-packages-ai.sh with
improved error handling and LLM-based fixes.

commit.message:
fix: debounce resize handlers to prevent WebGL crash on orientation change

Root cause: Portrait→landscape rotation fires 5-20+ rapid resize events.
Multiple competing handlers (makeRenderer, CanvasResizeHandler, R3F Canvas)
all called renderer.setSize() with conflicting dimensions, and CSS media
queries changed .scene-shell layout mid-stream causing getBoundingClientRect()
to return stale/zero values. This caused WebGL context loss on 2nd+ rotation.

Fixes:
- Rewrote CanvasResizeHandler: removed dead code with undefined sceneShell
  variable, added proper debounce (300ms mobile / 100ms desktop), added
  orientationchange listener, added visualViewport listener for iOS
- Added debounce to makeRenderer resize (300ms mobile / 50ms desktop)
- Changed R3F Canvas resize debounce from 0ms to 300ms on mobile
- All three handlers now wait for CSS layout to settle before reading dims

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use sql.join for array params in absence queries (ROK-535)

query failures. Switch to IN (sql.join(...)) to match the pattern
used elsewhere in the codebase. Also fixes the same latent bug in
GameAffinityNotificationService.

commit.message:
Add theme support for fonts, radius, and shared variables

- Extend Theme interface with sharedVariables (mode-independent: fonts,
  radius, shadows)
- Pre-load Outfit, Fira Code, Inter fonts in root layout alongside Geist
- Theme context applies sharedVariables and clears them on theme switch
- Add "Neon" theme (magenta primary, cyan accent, Outfit + Fira Code fonts)
- Theme preview renders custom font when theme specifies one

Themes can now control --font-sans, --font-mono, --radius, and any other
CSS variable via sharedVariables. Fonts are pre-loaded via next/font for
optimization, and themes reference them as var(--font-outfit) etc.

fix: make all notification inserts failure-safe with EXCEPTION blocks
